title: HW2 Q2 — $T(n) = 2T(n/3) + n$
statement: For the recurrence $T(n) = 2T(n/3) + n$ with $T(1) = 1$: (a) draw the recursion tree and find an asymptotically tight bound. (b) prove the $\Theta$-bound by induction.

steps.title: (a) Recursion tree.
steps.body:
At level $i$: $2^i$ subproblems of size $n/3^i$, non-recursive work per subproblem $= n/3^i$. Level work $= 2^i \cdot n/3^i = n \cdot (2/3)^i$.

This is decreasing geometric with ratio $2/3$. Sum:

$$\sum_{i=0}^{\log_3 n - 1} n (2/3)^i \le n \sum_{i=0}^{\infty} (2/3)^i = n \cdot \dfrac{1}{1 - 2/3} = 3n.$$

Leaves: $2^{\log_3 n} = n^{\log_3 2} \approx n^{0.63}$, each cost $1$. Leaf contribution $= n^{\log_3 2} = o(n)$.

**Total: $T(n) = \Theta(n)$.**

tips.label: Leaves are negligible
tips.content: $n^{\log_3 2}$ has exponent $< 1$, so it's $o(n)$ and absorbed by the $\Theta(n)$ bound.

steps.title: (b) Inductive upper bound $T(n) \le 3n$.
steps.body:
**Claim:** $T(n) \le 3n$ for all $n \ge 1$.

**Base:** $T(1) = 1 \le 3$. ✓

**I.H.:** For all $k < n$, $T(k) \le 3k$.

**Step:** $T(n) = 2T(n/3) + n \le 2 \cdot 3 \cdot (n/3) + n = 2n + n = 3n$. ✓

So $T(n) = O(n)$.

steps.title: (b) Inductive lower bound $T(n) \ge n$.
steps.body:
Trivially $T(n) = 2T(n/3) + n \ge n$ from the recurrence itself (since $T$ is positive). So $T(n) = \Omega(n)$.

Combining: $T(n) = \Theta(n)$. $\square$
